description_long: This is a large, multi-wing school building complex with a mix of flat and slightly sloped roofs, featuring several interconnected rectangular structures. The main building appears to be centrally located, with a prominent entrance and multiple stories, while the surrounding wings are single-story and extend outward, forming a sprawling layout. The exterior surfaces are primarily light-colored, with some sections in muted red and green tones, and the roofs are a combination of white, green, and brown. There are large paved areas adjacent to the building, likely serving as parking lots or playgrounds, and a few smaller outbuildings or annexes are visible. Windows are regularly spaced along the main building, and there are several access points and possible loading zones. The overall design is functional and institutional, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century educational facilities.
description: A sprawling, multi-wing school building with flat and sloped roofs, light-colored walls, and large paved areas, typical of institutional educational architecture.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance and central multi-story section, with large paved areas and the main wings extending to the sides.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the elongated layout, the green and brown roof sections, and the extensive paved area to the left.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back of the main building, additional annexes, and the full extent of the paved and green-roofed areas.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the main entrance, the central building's height, and the arrangement of the surrounding wings and paved zones.
